## [00:00 - 03:01]

Hi, this is Don Crowther. Let's talk about the Cells Page Promo process. So you've seen this before, we're driving traffic in, we're running them through the webinar registration, then we promote them to attend the webinar, and then after that, we do a replay promo. After that is where this Cells Page Promo happens to those who didn't buy during the replay. So basically what we're doing here is two things. First off, we're closing the cell for those who have participated in the webinar funnel, but still haven't purchased. And we're attempting to appeal to those oftentimes this will be the first time they will have even seen your offer, who won't watch a webinar or a video, which is still a very large percentage of people, it's more than half of your audience. So what we're doing here is a classic product launch formula, open cart period strategy. I'm going to review that here in this video, but I'm going to review in one video in a very few minutes, what Jeff Walker takes a whole module or two to talk about in that program. And so if you want to understand really how to nail this open cart period, I would suggest that you invest in product launch formula. It's just an invaluable piece of training about this really key period of time in any launch, whether it's a regular launch or a webinar launch. Now, as I said, this is a classic open cart period with one exception, which is in in product launch formula, they've been through an entire prelaunch content sequence with emails and videos before they even see the sales page. And so in this case, this group, some about half of your people will have never seen any of the prelaunch materials. So they haven't seen your webinar, they haven't seen your replay. And so the first exposure they're going to have to this process is this sales page. And so consequently, because the communications we make during the cart open period haven't happened, this particular video and sales page has to work extra hard. So key communications that need to happen in this period of the promo. First one, here's this cool product, and you can buy it now. Now remember, all your previous communications have talked about either the webinar or the replay, or in some cases, the opt in incentive around it, not the product itself. So we need to specifically introduce the product and the benefit it offers. Because if they're part of that 50% or so that haven't seen

## [03:01 - 06:07]

anything, this is the first time they're hearing hearing about things. So second key communication is to give them proof that this product works. This can be done through testimonials through case studies, and all kinds of engagement that show these are the results that other people who have who have used this program have achieved in their business. Next key communication is answers to their key questions. So their top questions, you also and by the way, this is a good idea is to offer a Q&A webinar or office hours so that they can ask questions directly of you. Most of the time they're not actually asking questions. What they're doing is they're saying, solve my objections, push me that little bit over the top so that I am able to buy your product. Next key communication that needs to occur during this period is here's another reason to buy. And so it could be adding another bonus. And of course, anyone who purchased earlier will get it, but that way you're giving them another reason to buy in case they're almost there, but just not pushed over the edge, or they made the decision not to buy. And now we're going to send them another reason to purchase adding another bonus that they really, really want so that they will reconsider that purchase, not the decision not to purchase. Next key communication, hey, the carts carts closing, and this puts an urgency because they need to make the decision now. Now each of these key communications is done through email. So you're sending out an email for each one of these five different key communications in this process, but nothing says these have to be single emails. For example, we're going to definitely want to send three separate emails on the cart close day one in the morning, one in the afternoon, and then one just two or three hours before cart close, well three or four hours before cart close, just to say, Hey, this is really, really your last chance. So your mission here in this series of emails and other things you're going to do during this promotional period is to get them to visit the sales page to absorb the information there and to buy. Also, it's to revisit the sales page if they went before, and they didn't buy. So realize that oftentimes, especially if it's a high dollar purchase, I'll just say over $1,000, people need a couple of invitations to purchase. And oftentimes they end up going to a sales page a couple of times before they actually invest. They'll go once they'll say, I'm somewhat interested, maybe I'll buy later,

## [06:07 - 06:56]

or maybe they go when they say, I'm somewhat interested, I'm not going to buy. If you give them repeated opportunities to come to that page and look at it, they're more likely to actually buy in those subsequent visits. Bonus tip. Whenever someone actually buys, remove them from the promotional sequences, these other emails that they're getting, you don't want to keep reminding them that the product is still for sale. You don't want to send them three emails on the last day if they already bought on the first day, they don't need to get all that. And so just remove them from the emails that are going out once they buy so they don't continue to receive any more emails during that promotional sequence.
